title:
EEG: Electrophysiological biomarkers of behavioral dimensions 
from cross-species paradigms
description: This dataset comprises electrophysiological recordings from 57 human subjects performing three different behavioral tasks designed to identify cross-species electrophysiological biomarkers of behavioral dimensions. The study integrates human EEG data with complementary mouse neurophysiology to establish translational biomarkers of behavior, with data collection occurring between 2014-2016 and analysis between 2015-2021.
methods_description: EEG data were collected from 57 human subjects performing three different tasks. Trigger signals were encoded as odd binary recombinations and subsequently translated to 0-255 values using custom MATLAB scripts. Data collection was conducted in San Diego, California.
